fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with high revenue expansion but currently unprofitable and trading at a high forward multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter reported EPS of -0.07 vs estimate -0.05; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — significant future growth expected.
Forward P/E 35.60x, PEG ratio 0.78 — growth may justify the multiple.
Current ratio 3.83, Quick ratio 2.85 — healthy short-term liquidity.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6%, Profit margin -8.5% — currently unprofitable.
Return on equity -6.1%, Return on assets -3.3% — poor returns on capital.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant positive attention recently, with articles highlighting major transceiver orders and a rapidly shifting story. Despite a substantial 6-month stock surge, some analysts remain long, while others question if it's a bubble. The company's growth trajectory, particularly its analyst growth estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year, is a key focus.
